file.content:
# Loyalty App

A B2B2C digital loyalty platform — mobile app (consumer) + web dashboard (merchant) + Supabase backend.

## Structure

- `apps/mobile` — React Native (Expo) consumer app
- `apps/dashboard` — Next.js merchant dashboard
- `packages/shared` — Shared TypeScript types
- `supabase/` — Database migrations + Edge Functions

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites
- Node.js 20+
- pnpm 9+
- Supabase CLI (optional, for local dev)

### Install
```bash
pnpm install
```

### Development
```bash
# Dashboard
pnpm --filter dashboard dev

# Mobile
cd apps/mobile && npx expo start
```

### Environment Variables

Copy `.env.example` files in `apps/dashboard/` and configure:
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`
- `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`

## Tech Stack

- **Mobile:** React Native + Expo SDK 54, Expo Router, Zustand, i18next
- **Dashboard:** Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui
- **Backend:** Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Edge Functions, Storage)
- **Payments:** Stripe
- **Push:** Expo Notifications

input.description: List apps, packages, and supabase directories

dashboard
mobile
---
shared
---
config.toml
functions
migrations

content.text:
This is a **B2B2C digital loyalty platform** monorepo managed with **pnpm workspaces + Turborepo**. Here's the structure:

| Component | Tech | Path |
|---|---|---|
| **Mobile app** (consumer) | React Native + Expo SDK 54 | `apps/mobile` |
| **Dashboard** (merchant) | Next.js 15 + Tailwind + shadcn/ui | `apps/dashboard` |
| **Shared types** | TypeScript | `packages/shared` |
| **Backend** | Supabase (Postgres, Auth, Edge Functions) | `supabase/` |
| **Payments** | Stripe | — |

What would you like to do with this project?

data.prompt: The user asks: "How to activate remote control in Claude Code?" (comment activer le remote control). Please explain what remote control is in Claude Code, how to enable it, and how to use it. Include any relevant CLI flags, settings, or configuration steps.

content.content:
> ## Documentation Index
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://code.claude.com/docs/llms.txt
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

# Continue local sessions from any device with Remote Control

> Continue a local Claude Code session from your phone, tablet, or any browser using Remote Control. Works with claude.ai/code and the Claude mobile app.

<Note>
  Remote Control is available as a research preview on Pro and Max plans. It is not available on Team or Enterprise plans.
</Note>

Remote Control connects [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code) or the Claude app for [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) and [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude) to a Claude Code session running on your machine. Start a task at your desk, then pick it up from your phone on the couch or a browser on another computer.

When you start a Remote Control session on your machine, Claude keeps running locally the entire time, so nothing moves to the cloud. With Remote Control you can:

* **Use your full local environment remotely**: your filesystem, [MCP servers](/en/mcp), tools, and project configuration all stay available
* **Work from both surfaces at once**: the conversation stays in sync across all connected devices, so you can send messages from your terminal, browser, and phone interchangeably
* **Survive interruptions**: if your laptop sleeps or your network drops, the session reconnects automatically when your machine comes back online

Unlike [Claude Code on the web](/en/claude-code-on-the-web), which runs on cloud infrastructure, Remote Control sessions run directly on your machine and interact with your local filesystem. The web and mobile interfaces are just a window into that local session.

This page covers setup, how to start and connect to sessions, and how Remote Control compares to Claude Code on the web.

## Requirements

Before using Remote Control, confirm that your environment meets these conditions:

* **Subscription**: requires a Pro or Max plan. API keys are not supported.
* **Authentication**: run `claude` and use `/login` to sign in through claude.ai if you haven't already.
* **Workspace trust**: run `claude` in your project directory at least once to accept the workspace trust dialog.

## Start a Remote Control session

You can start a new session directly in Remote Control, or connect a session that's already running.

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="New session">
    Navigate to your project directory and run:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    claude remote-control
    ```

    The process stays running in your terminal, waiting for remote connections. It displays a session URL you can use to [connect from another device](#connect-from-another-device), and you can press spacebar to show a QR code for quick access from your phone. While a remote session is active, the terminal shows connection status and tool activity.

    This command supports the following flags:

    * **`--verbose`**: show detailed connection and session logs
    * **`--sandbox`** / **`--no-sandbox`**: enable or disable [sandboxing](/en/sandboxing) for filesystem and network isolation during the session. Sandboxing is off by default.
  </Tab>

  <Tab title="From an existing session">
    If you're already in a Claude Code session and want to continue it remotely, use the `/remote-control` (or `/rc`) command:

    ```
    /remote-control
    ```

    This starts a Remote Control session that carries over your current conversation history and displays a session URL and QR code you can use to [connect from another device](#connect-from-another-device). The `--verbose`, `--sandbox`, and `--no-sandbox` flags are not available with this command.

    <Tip>
      Use `/rename` before running `/remote-control` to give the session a descriptive name. This makes it easier to find in the session list across devices.
    </Tip>
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

### Connect from another device

Once a Remote Control session is active, you have a few ways to connect from another device:

* **Open the session URL** in any browser to go directly to the session on [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code). Both `claude remote-control` and `/remote-control` display this URL in the terminal.
* **Scan the QR code** shown alongside the session URL to open it directly in the Claude app. With `claude remote-control`, press spacebar to toggle the QR code display.
* **Open [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code) or the Claude app** and find the session by name in the session list. Remote Control sessions show a computer icon with a green status dot when online.

The remote session takes its name from your last message, your `/rename` value, or "Remote Control session" if there's no conversation history. If the environment already has an active session, you'll be asked whether to continue it or start a new one.

If you don't have the Claude app yet, use the `/mobile` command inside Claude Code to display a download QR code for [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) or [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude).

### Enable Remote Control for all sessions

By default, Remote Control only activates when you explicitly run `claude remote-control` or `/remote-control`. To enable it automatically for every session, run `/config` inside Claude Code and set **Enable Remote Control for all sessions** to `true`. Set it back to `false` to disable.

Each Claude Code instance supports one remote session at a time. If you run multiple instances, each one gets its own environment and session.

## Connection and security

Your local Claude Code session makes outbound HTTPS requests only and never opens inbound ports on your machine. When you start Remote Control, it registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work. When you connect from another device, the server routes messages between the web or mobile client and your local session over a streaming connection.

All traffic travels through the Anthropic API over TLS, the same transport security as any Claude Code session. The connection uses multiple short-lived credentials, each scoped to a single purpose and expiring independently.

## Remote Control vs Claude Code on the web

Remote Control and [Claude Code on the web](/en/claude-code-on-the-web) both use the claude.ai/code interface. The key difference is where the session runs: Remote Control executes on your machine, so your local MCP servers, tools, and project configuration stay available. Claude Code on the web executes in Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure.

Use Remote Control when you're in the middle of local work and want to keep going from another device. Use Claude Code on the web when you want to kick off a task without any local setup, work on a repo you don't have cloned, or run multiple tasks in parallel.

## Limitations

* **One remote session at a time**: each Claude Code session supports one remote connection.
* **Terminal must stay open**: Remote Control runs as a local process. If you close the terminal or stop the `claude` process, the session ends. Run `claude remote-control` again to start a new one.
* **Extended network outage**: if your machine is awake but unable to reach the network for more than roughly 10 minutes, the session times out and the process exits. Run `claude remote-control` again to start a new session.

## Related resources

* [Claude Code on the web](/en/claude-code-on-the-web): run sessions in Anthropic-managed cloud environments instead of on your machine
* [Authentication](/en/authentication): set up `/login` and manage credentials for claude.ai
* [CLI reference](/en/cli-reference): full list of flags and commands including `claude remote-control`
* [Security](/en/security): how Remote Control sessions fit into the Claude Code security model
* [Data usage](/en/data-usage): what data flows through the Anthropic API during local and remote sessions

content.text:
Perfect! Here's a complete guide to activating and using Remote Control in Claude Code:

## What is Remote Control?

Remote Control allows you to continue a Claude Code session running on your local machine from any other device—your phone, tablet, or another computer via a browser. Your local environment, files, and MCP servers remain on your machine while you control it remotely. Unlike Claude Code on the web (which runs in the cloud), Remote Control keeps everything local and just provides remote access.

## Requirements

Before you can use Remote Control, verify:

1. **Subscription**: Pro or Max plan (not available on Team/Enterprise plans)
2. **Authentication**: Run `claude` and use `/login` to authenticate through claude.ai
3. **Workspace trust**: Run `claude` in your project directory at least once to accept the workspace trust dialog

## Activation Methods

### Method 1: Start a New Remote Control Session

Navigate to your project directory and run:

```bash
claude remote-control
```

**Flags available:**
- `--verbose` - Show detailed connection and session logs
- `--sandbox` / `--no-sandbox` - Enable/disable sandboxing for filesystem and network isolation (off by default)

The process will display a session URL and QR code you can share. Press spacebar to toggle the QR code display.

### Method 2: Convert Existing Session to Remote Control

If you're already in a Claude Code session, use:

```
/remote-control
```

or the shorthand:

```
/rc
```

This carries over your conversation history and displays the session URL and QR code.

**Tip**: Use `/rename` before running `/remote-control` to give the session a descriptive name for easier identification across devices.

### Method 3: Enable Remote Control for All Sessions

To automatically enable Remote Control for every new session:

1. Run `/config` inside Claude Code
2. Set **"Enable Remote Control for all sessions"** to `true`
3. Set to `false` to disable later

## Connecting from Another Device

Once your Remote Control session is active, connect via:

1. **Session URL** - Open the URL in any browser to access claude.ai/code directly
2. **QR Code** - Scan with your phone to open in the Claude mobile app (iOS/Android)
3. **Session List** - Open claude.ai/code or the Claude app and find your Remote Control session in the list (shows a computer icon with a green status dot when online)

**Get the Claude mobile app:**
Run `/mobile` inside Claude Code to display download QR codes for [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) or [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude).

## Key Features

- **Full local environment accessible**: Your filesystem, MCP servers, tools, and project configuration all work remotely
- **Work from multiple surfaces**: Send messages from terminal, browser, and phone interchangeably
- **Auto-reconnect**: If your laptop sleeps or network drops, the session reconnects automatically when your machine comes back online
- **Secure**: All traffic uses TLS over the Anthropic API with short-lived, scoped credentials

## Important Limitations

1. **One remote session at a time** - Each Claude Code instance supports only one remote connection
2. **Terminal must stay open** - The `claude remote-control` process must continue running
3. **Network timeout** - If your machine loses network connectivity for ~10 minutes, the session times out and you must restart

## Session Naming

Remote Control sessions take their name from:
1. Your `/rename` value (recommended)
2. Your last message
3. Default: "Remote Control session"

This makes it easier to find sessions across devices.

For detailed documentation, see the [Remote Control documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control.md).

toolUseResult.prompt: The user asks: "How to activate remote control in Claude Code?" (comment activer le remote control). Please explain what remote control is in Claude Code, how to enable it, and how to use it. Include any relevant CLI flags, settings, or configuration steps.

content.text:
Perfect! Here's a complete guide to activating and using Remote Control in Claude Code:

## What is Remote Control?

Remote Control allows you to continue a Claude Code session running on your local machine from any other device—your phone, tablet, or another computer via a browser. Your local environment, files, and MCP servers remain on your machine while you control it remotely. Unlike Claude Code on the web (which runs in the cloud), Remote Control keeps everything local and just provides remote access.

## Requirements

Before you can use Remote Control, verify:

1. **Subscription**: Pro or Max plan (not available on Team/Enterprise plans)
2. **Authentication**: Run `claude` and use `/login` to authenticate through claude.ai
3. **Workspace trust**: Run `claude` in your project directory at least once to accept the workspace trust dialog

## Activation Methods

### Method 1: Start a New Remote Control Session

Navigate to your project directory and run:

```bash
claude remote-control
```

**Flags available:**
- `--verbose` - Show detailed connection and session logs
- `--sandbox` / `--no-sandbox` - Enable/disable sandboxing for filesystem and network isolation (off by default)

The process will display a session URL and QR code you can share. Press spacebar to toggle the QR code display.

### Method 2: Convert Existing Session to Remote Control

If you're already in a Claude Code session, use:

```
/remote-control
```

or the shorthand:

```
/rc
```

This carries over your conversation history and displays the session URL and QR code.

**Tip**: Use `/rename` before running `/remote-control` to give the session a descriptive name for easier identification across devices.

### Method 3: Enable Remote Control for All Sessions

To automatically enable Remote Control for every new session:

1. Run `/config` inside Claude Code
2. Set **"Enable Remote Control for all sessions"** to `true`
3. Set to `false` to disable later

## Connecting from Another Device

Once your Remote Control session is active, connect via:

1. **Session URL** - Open the URL in any browser to access claude.ai/code directly
2. **QR Code** - Scan with your phone to open in the Claude mobile app (iOS/Android)
3. **Session List** - Open claude.ai/code or the Claude app and find your Remote Control session in the list (shows a computer icon with a green status dot when online)

**Get the Claude mobile app:**
Run `/mobile` inside Claude Code to display download QR codes for [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) or [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude).

## Key Features

- **Full local environment accessible**: Your filesystem, MCP servers, tools, and project configuration all work remotely
- **Work from multiple surfaces**: Send messages from terminal, browser, and phone interchangeably
- **Auto-reconnect**: If your laptop sleeps or network drops, the session reconnects automatically when your machine comes back online
- **Secure**: All traffic uses TLS over the Anthropic API with short-lived, scoped credentials

## Important Limitations

1. **One remote session at a time** - Each Claude Code instance supports only one remote connection
2. **Terminal must stay open** - The `claude remote-control` process must continue running
3. **Network timeout** - If your machine loses network connectivity for ~10 minutes, the session times out and you must restart

## Session Naming

Remote Control sessions take their name from:
1. Your `/rename` value (recommended)
2. Your last message
3. Default: "Remote Control session"

This makes it easier to find sessions across devices.

For detailed documentation, see the [Remote Control documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control.md).

## Activer le Remote Control dans Claude Code

### Prérequis
- Abonnement **Pro** ou **Max** (pas disponible sur Team/Enterprise)
- Être authentifié via `/login`

---

### 3 façons de l'activer

**1. Nouvelle session remote :**
```bash
claude remote-control
```

**2. Depuis une session existante :**
```
/remote-control
```
ou raccourci : `/rc`

**3. Activer pour toutes les sessions :**
- Lancer `/config`
- Mettre **"Enable Remote Control for all sessions"** à `true`

---

### Se connecter depuis un autre appareil

Une fois activé, un **URL** et un **QR code** s'affichent. Vous pouvez :
- Ouvrir l'URL dans un navigateur sur **claude.ai/code**
- Scanner le QR code avec l'app mobile Claude (iOS/Android)
- Retrouver la session dans la liste sur claude.ai/code (icône PC avec point vert)

### Points importants
- Le terminal local doit rester ouvert
- Une seule session remote à la fois
- Reconnexion automatique si le réseau coupe brièvement (~10 min max de timeout)

Vous voulez que je l'active maintenant avec `/rc` ?

Unfortunately, I can't run `/remote-control` for you — it's an interactive CLI command that must be executed directly in your terminal.

Run this in your terminal :

```bash
claude remote-control
```

Ou si vous êtes déjà dans une session Claude Code, tapez :

```
/rc
```

Un QR code et une URL s'afficheront pour vous connecter depuis un autre appareil.

La version 2.1.59 est récente, le remote control devrait être disponible.

Vérifiez les points suivants :

1. **Êtes-vous authentifié ?** Tapez `/login` dans Claude Code pour vérifier votre connexion à claude.ai

2. **Avez-vous un abonnement Pro ou Max ?** Le remote control n'est pas disponible sur les plans gratuits ou Team/Enterprise

3. **Essayez directement depuis un nouveau terminal :**
   ```bash
   claude remote-control --verbose
   ```
   Le flag `--verbose` affichera plus de détails sur ce qui se passe.

Si rien ne s'affiche du tout (même pas d'erreur), c'est probablement un problème d'abonnement. Quel plan Claude utilisez-vous ?

content.thinking: The user is typing the command but they need to run it in their actual terminal, not here in our conversation. I should clarify this.

content.text:
Cette commande doit être exécutée **directement dans un nouveau terminal Windows** (PowerShell ou CMD), pas ici dans notre conversation.

Ouvrez un nouveau terminal et lancez :

```bash
claude remote-control --verbose
```

Si vous obtenez une erreur ou un message, copiez-collez le ici et je vous aiderai à résoudre le problème.

content: donne moi l'url du remote control

message.content: donne moi l'url du remote control

content.text:
Je n'ai pas accès à l'URL du remote control — elle est **générée dynamiquement** à chaque session et s'affiche uniquement dans votre terminal local.

Je ne peux pas la générer ni la retrouver pour vous. Vous devez :

1. Ouvrir **un nouveau terminal PowerShell** (en dehors de cette session)
2. Taper :
   ```bash
   claude remote-control
   ```
3. L'URL et le QR code s'afficheront directement dans ce terminal

Sinon, vous pouvez aussi aller sur **https://claude.ai/code** dans votre navigateur — si une session remote est active, elle apparaîtra dans la liste.

content.text:
Cette commande aussi doit être exécutée dans un **terminal séparé**, pas ici.

Ouvrez un nouveau PowerShell et tapez :

```bash
claude logout
```

Puis pour vous reconnecter :

```bash
claude login
```

Voulez-vous de l'aide sur autre chose concernant le projet loyalty-app ?
